summary: mnemonic-safety-check exfiltrates host data and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package executes a host-information beacon at install time via package.json line 30 and on use via scanner.js. The lifecycle script and scanner.js gather process environment variables, working directory, OS hostname, and the output of `whoami` (via require('child_process') execSync) and POST them to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js (~270 lines) combines fs.existsSync probes of filesystem paths with https.request/https.get calls to the same domain, consistent with a credential/host-recon exfiltration tool rather than a legitimate 'mnemonic safety' utility. The package name plus the system-intel exfiltration shape (env vars, hostname, whoami, cwd shipped to a static external host) constitutes an active supply-chain attack on installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
